canonical_question: How do I implement topological sort and when to use it?
constraints: Input graph MUST be a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG); topological sort is undefined for graphs with cycles
A DAG may have multiple valid topological orderings; algorithms return one valid ordering unless explicitly enumerating all
Always run cycle detection before or during topological sort; silently ignoring cycles produces incorrect orderings
All-orderings enumeration is O(n! * V) worst case; never enumerate on graphs with more than ~20 vertices without pruning

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Graph is undirected or contains cycles
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use cycle detection (DFS back-edge) first, or use strongly connected components (Tarjan/Kosaraju)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need shortest/longest path in weighted DAG
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use DAG relaxation with topological order (single-source shortest path in DAG)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to find connected components, not ordering

inputs_needed.question: Do you prefer BFS-based (Kahn's) or DFS-based topological sort?
Do you need all valid topological orderings or just one?
Does your input graph need cycle detection?
